marketPulse: Relief Rally within Volatile Macro Shock
marketPulseSubtext: Oil down, stocks higher, volatility easing, but rates and gold still signal unresolved macro stress
topDriver: War de-escalation hopes + inflation still unresolved
topDriverSubtext: Oil cooled enough to lift risk assets, but pressure is still visible across rates, gold, and broader macro signals
disclaimer: Prototype disclaimer: This screen reflects a manual April 1, 2026 market snapshot, but it is not validated for trading, investment, or risk decisions. It would need to be hooked up to real-time market and news sources, then scored by tested algorithms and monitoring before production use.

topThemes.theme: Rates stay firm enough to matter
Safety demand remains underneath the bounce

rateCurveRead: As of the April 1 snapshot, Treasury yields were still firm rather than collapsing, which suggests the market is treating the easing in oil as helpful but not enough to fully erase inflation and growth concerns.

crossMarket.driver: Broad relief rally as de-escalation hopes improve sentiment
Growth rebounds as oil pressure cools and tech leads
Cyclicals stabilize as war-risk premium eases
Safe-haven demand fades as headline stress cools
Fed path still uncertain, cuts not fully back in control
Inflation and growth still in tension
War-risk premium fades on de-escalation hopes
Crude reprices lower, but still near triple digits
Safety bid remains active even as stocks rebound
Fear eases, but the market is not back to calm
crossMarket.trend: Lowest in more than a week, still above normal

crossMarket.driver: Speculative appetite steadies with risk sentiment

drivers.name: Oil is still the first lens, but no longer the only one
drivers.detail: Crude fell sharply on hopes the Iran war could de-escalate, but prices are still elevated enough to keep inflation and growth concerns alive.

drivers.name: The relief rally is real, but still tactical
drivers.detail: U.S. stocks rallied for a second straight session, led by tech, but the bounce still depends heavily on shifting geopolitical headlines.

drivers.name: Bond yields are not behaving like a full all-clear
drivers.detail: Treasury yields stayed firm rather than collapsing, which suggests inflation repricing and resilient economic data are still limiting the bond market's safe-haven response.

drivers.name: Gold is confirming that caution remains underneath the surface
drivers.detail: Gold kept climbing even as stocks rallied, showing that investors are still paying for protection rather than embracing a clean risk-on reset.

drivers.name: Other areas to watch now are rates, volatility, shipping, and consumer spillover
drivers.detail: Beyond oil, the next important read-throughs are Treasury yields, the VIX, fuel and shipping-related pressure, and whether higher energy costs spill into housing and consumer demand.
drivers.linked: VIX, Treasury curve, mortgage rates, transports, consumer sensitivity

risks.title: Rates stay too high for comfort
risks.desc: If yields remain firm while oil holds near $100, financial conditions can stay tighter than equity bulls want.

risks.title: Volatility cools, but not enough
risks.desc: A VIX near the mid-20s is better than 30-plus, but it still leaves the market vulnerable to sharp headline reactions.

risks.title: Consumer and housing spillover
risks.desc: Higher mortgage rates and fuel costs could still start weighing on affordability, travel, and discretionary demand.

risks.title: False dawn on de-escalation
risks.desc: If the war narrative reverses again, oil, gold, and volatility could all snap back higher quickly.

timeline.headline: Global markets rallied on hopes the Iran war could de-escalate within weeks.
timeline.effect: Risk appetite improved and oil sold off from prior panic levels.

timeline.headline: Crude inventories rose sharply in the U.S. and helped reinforce the move lower in oil.
timeline.effect: Energy pressure eased and the market leaned further into the relief trade.

timeline.headline: U.S. stocks finished higher again, led by technology and growth shares.
timeline.effect: The rally broadened, but gains still rested on fragile geopolitical optimism.

timeline.headline: Gold stayed strong and volatility remained elevated despite the rally.
timeline.effect: The close still signaled caution underneath the bounce rather than a full reset to calm.

strategies.title: Watch Rates With Oil
strategies.body: The cleanest test of whether this bounce can hold is not just lower crude, but lower or at least stable Treasury yields alongside it.
strategies.trigger: WTI stays near or below $100 and 10Y does not re-accelerate higher

strategies.title: Monitor Volatility Reset
strategies.body: A falling VIX would support the rally, but if volatility stalls in the mid-20s, markets may still be vulnerable to failed follow-through.
strategies.trigger: VIX holds below roughly 25 or turns back up

strategies.title: Respect Gold's Message
strategies.body: If gold keeps rising while equities also rise, the market may be pricing tactical relief without fully trusting the geopolitical or inflation backdrop.
strategies.trigger: Gold remains firm even as stocks extend gains

strategies.title: Watch Housing and Consumer Pressure
strategies.body: Mortgage rates, travel-sensitive names, and discretionary spending are useful next-leg indicators for whether the oil shock starts showing up in the real economy.
strategies.trigger: Mortgage rates stay high and consumer-sensitive groups lag
